rationale: Mention A is the general concept of a path, while mention B is a specific problem (single-source shortest-path) that uses paths. They are at different granularities and denote distinct objects.

evidence_spans.span: A weighted directed graph assigns a numerical weight \(w(u,v)\) to each directed edge \((u,v)\). A path is a sequence of compatible directed edges. Its path cost is the sum of their edge weights:

The single-source shortest-path problem asks for a minimum-cost path from a chosen source vertex to every reachable vertex. Dijkstra's algorithm is applied to this problem when all edge weights are nonnegative.
